stories.title: NASA chief says 90% chance of proving ancient life on Mars
stories.description: Jared Isaacman pointed to the universe's scale and recent Perseverance rover findings as reasons for optimism about microbial evidence on Mars.

stories.title: Trump claims Iran requested ceasefire; Tehran denies it
stories.description: Hours before a planned primetime address on the war, Trump and Iran offered starkly conflicting accounts of ceasefire talks.

stories.title: Iran hits tanker off Qatar, strikes Kuwait airport as Gulf war widens
stories.description: Qatar said it intercepted two of three cruise missiles from Iran, but a third struck a QatarEnergy-chartered tanker. No casualties were reported.

stories.title: NASA set to launch Artemis II moon mission today
stories.description: The first crewed lunar voyage since 1972 is targeting liftoff at 6:24 p.m. EDT from Kennedy Space Center with four astronauts aboard.

stories.title: South Korea exports hit record $86B as global factories stockpile amid Iran war
stories.description: Semiconductor shipments surged over 150%, while euro zone and U.S. factory activity beat forecasts as firms panic-bought materials ahead of further disruptions.

stories.title: Qubits needed to crack encryption fall 200-fold in under a year
stories.description: Three papers have slashed the estimated quantum computing power needed to break RSA-2048 from 20 million qubits to under 100,000, accelerating the threat to modern encryption.

stories.title: Global stocks surge as Trump signals end to Iran war
stories.description: European and Indian markets rallied sharply on Wednesday after Trump said U.S. forces could withdraw from Iran within weeks.

stories.title: Parker Solar Probe data challenges models of Sun's magnetic engine
stories.description: Protons and heavy ions behave differently during solar magnetic reconnection, contradicting models that assumed uniform particle acceleration.

stories.title: USTR flags Canadian alcohol bans, 'Buy Canadian' as trade barriers
stories.description: The annual report demands American liquor "immediately and permanently" return to provincial shelves ahead of the USMCA's first formal review in July.

stories.title: New papers slash qubit estimates for codebreaking
stories.description: The March 31 papers sharply cut projected quantum resources needed to crack elliptic curve encryption, intensifying pressure to adopt post-quantum safeguards.

stories.title: DHS pauses warehouse purchases for immigrant detention
stories.description: New Secretary Mullin is reviewing the $38.3 billion detention expansion program launched by his predecessor, Kristi Noem.

stories.title: Forest Service to move headquarters to Salt Lake City in sweeping overhaul
stories.description: The USDA announced the agency's nine regional offices will close and be replaced by 15 state directors under a new organizational model.

stories.title: China and Pakistan propose ceasefire as Chinese ships transit Hormuz
stories.description: Beijing confirmed three Chinese vessels crossed the strait after coordinating with Tehran, as China and Pakistan unveiled a five-point peace plan.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel destroying Iran's weapons capability
stories.description: In a Passover eve address, the prime minister said Israel had inflicted "10 plagues" on Iran and its regional proxy networks.

stories.title: USS Bush strike group deploys as Hegseth calls Iran war days 'decisive'
stories.description: The carrier left Norfolk on Tuesday as the Pentagon said U.S. forces have struck over 11,000 targets in the one-month-old campaign against Iran.

stories.title: Syria vows to stay out of US-Israeli war on Iran unless attacked
stories.description: President Ahmed al-Sharaa declared neutrality in a London speech, saying Syria has "had enough war" as the conflict enters its second month.

stories.title: UN warns Lebanon at 'breaking point' as 1.1M displaced
stories.description: Emergency Relief Coordinator Tom Fletcher told the Security Council that over 1,240 people have been killed in four weeks as Israeli operations intensify.

stories.title: Trump says Strait of Hormuz will 'automatically' reopen when US exits Iran war
stories.description: The president told the New York Post the war "won't last much longer," but Iran denied any negotiations are underway to end the conflict.

stories.title: Judge blocks deal that would let churches endorse candidates
stories.description: A federal judge in Texas rejected a proposed IRS settlement that would have exempted houses of worship from the Johnson Amendment's ban on political endorsements.

stories.title: FDA moves to allow banned peptides backed by RFK Jr.
stories.description: The agency is expected to reclassify more than a dozen injectable peptides restricted in 2023, restoring access through compounding pharmacies.

stories.title: Explosions rock Damascus as Israel intercepts Iranian missiles over Syria
stories.description: Syrian state TV reported blasts across the capital on Tuesday as Israeli air defenses shot down Iranian projectiles crossing Syrian airspace.

stories.title: Rubio directs US embassies to use X and military psyops against propaganda
stories.description: The diplomatic cable instructs staff worldwide to coordinate with Pentagon psychological operations and leverage Elon Musk's platform.

stories.title: Judge orders UPenn to hand over Jewish employee records
stories.description: The ruling gives Penn until May 1 to comply with the subpoena but bars disclosure of individuals' ties to specific Jewish organizations.

stories.title: Bipartisan senators introduce bill to boost Taiwan drone production
stories.description: The legislation would create a U.S.-Taiwan working group to co-produce drones and build supply chains independent from China.

stories.title: Trump signs order restricting mail-in voting ahead of midterms
stories.description: The executive order directs federal agencies to limit absentee ballot access, building on prior election actions largely blocked by courts.

stories.title: Fourth Greek tanker crosses Hormuz despite Iran blockade
stories.description: Dynacom Tankers sent the Suezmax Pola through the strait with its transponder off, carrying 1 million barrels of crude to Thailand.

stories.title: Pentagon says B-52s now flying over Iran after 11,000 strikes in 30 days
stories.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth and Gen. Caine said near-total destruction of Iran's air defenses enabled the first overland bomber missions.

stories.title: Majority disapprove of Trump's signature on dollar bills, YouGov poll finds
stories.description: The survey found 48% strongly oppose replacing the U.S. Treasurer's signature with the president's on new paper currency.

stories.title: Supreme Court strikes down Colorado conversion therapy ban in 8-1 ruling
stories.description: The decision in Chiles v. Salazar requires courts to apply heightened First Amendment scrutiny to laws restricting talk therapy for minors.

stories.title: Hegseth calls upcoming days 'decisive' in Iran war
stories.description: The defense secretary pointed to mass desertions and degraded Iranian military capabilities as signs the conflict is nearing a turning point.

stories.title: Judge permanently blocks Trump order defunding NPR, PBS
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Randolph Moss ruled the executive order violated the First Amendment by punishing the outlets over speech the president dislikes.

stories.title: Vance to publish book on Catholic faith while in office
stories.description: "Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith," due June 16, traces the vice president's journey from atheism to Catholicism.

stories.title: Global banks turn to Chinese stocks as Iran war roils markets
stories.description: Reuters reports major banks are treating Chinese equities as a safe haven, with renewable energy stocks surging even as global indexes tumble.

stories.title: Judge halts Trump's White House ballroom construction
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Richard Leon ruled that "no statute" authorizes the $400 million project, ordering work to stop unless Congress approves it.

stories.title: Australia, Indonesia probe tech giants over under-16 bans
stories.description: Both countries are investigating major platforms for failing to enforce social media age restrictions, with fines and sanctions on the table.

stories.title: Data centers warm nearby land by up to 16°F, Cambridge study finds
stories.description: Satellite data from roughly 6,000 facilities worldwide shows AI data centers create "heat islands" affecting an estimated 340 million people.

stories.title: Trump warns European allies after Italy, France block US military access
stories.description: Italy denied US bombers use of a Sicilian base, and France blocked Israeli overflights carrying American weapons, deepening a NATO rift over the Iran war.

stories.title: Pentagon denies Hegseth sought defense investments before Iran war
stories.description: The Financial Times reported that Hegseth's broker sought to invest millions in a defense ETF weeks before the Feb. 28 strike on Iran.

stories.title: NYU closes Abu Dhabi campus after Iran threatens US universities
stories.description: The IRGC declared American university campuses across the Gulf "legitimate targets," setting a Monday morning deadline for Washington to condemn strikes on Iranian schools.

stories.title: Noem's final day at DHS arrives amid IG probe, spending scandal
stories.description: Markwayne Mullin takes over as secretary while investigators examine contracts and lavish ad spending tied to Noem and adviser Corey Lewandowski.

stories.title: Health groups sue EPA over mercury rule rollback for coal plants
stories.description: A coalition of two dozen organizations filed suit in D.C. Circuit court, arguing the repeal of tighter emissions limits endangers children and communities.

stories.title: Zelensky says Russia exploiting Iran war to fund its fight against Ukraine
stories.description: In an exclusive interview, the Ukrainian president accused Moscow of sharing satellite imagery with Iran and profiting from loosened oil sanctions.

stories.title: Trump unveils 47-story skyscraper design for Miami presidential library
stories.description: The towering glass tower would break sharply from the low-slung, campus-style buildings that have defined presidential libraries for decades.

stories.title: Iran says ambassador will stay in Beirut despite Lebanon's expulsion order
stories.description: Tehran confirmed Monday its envoy remains in Lebanon past the March 29 departure deadline, deepening a diplomatic standoff backed by Hezbollah.

stories.title: Ukraine says Russia recruited 27,000 from 135 countries
stories.description: Ukrainian intelligence released data identifying 27,407 foreign nationals recruited into Russia's armed forces from 135 countries.

stories.title: US struck Isfahan ammo depot with bunker busters
stories.description: Trump shared footage of the massive secondary explosions on Truth Social as the month-old U.S.-Israeli campaign surpasses 15,000 targets struck.

stories.title: FBI labels Temple Isreal attack Hezbollah-inspired terrorism
stories.description: The bureau said Ayman Ghazali planned the March 12 ramming and shooting at Temple Israel to "kill as many" people as possible.

stories.title: Manhattan prosecutors probe insider trading on prediction markets
stories.description: The SDNY recently met with Polymarket to discuss how fraud and insider trading laws apply to suspicious bets on the fast-growing platforms.

stories.title: White House urges Congress back from recess amid DHS shutdown
stories.description: Trump urged Congress to cancel Easter recess and return to Washington, offering to host lawmakers for dinner at the White House.

stories.title: Israel Knesset passes death penalty law for Palestinians
stories.description: Military courts must now impose a mandatory death sentence for terrorism-related killings, with executions by hanging within 90 days of sentencing.

stories.title: NYT identifies untested U.S. missile in deadly Iran civilian strike
stories.description: A New York Times investigation found the Pentagon used a never-before-deployed Precision Strike Missile in a Feb. 28 strike that killed at least 21 people.

stories.title: Trump floats asking Arab states to help pay for Iran war
stories.description: The White House said Monday the president is interested in having Gulf allies share the financial burden of the conflict, now in its fifth week.

stories.title: Washington governor signs millionaires tax into law
stories.description: The 9.9% levy on household income above $1 million is the state's first personal income tax in nearly a century.

stories.title: Renoir, Cezanne, Matisse paintings stolen in 3-minute Italian museum heist
stories.description: Four masked thieves took three masterpieces worth an estimated €9 million from a private museum near Parma; investigators are reviewing surveillance footage.

stories.title: Trump confirms US is negotiating with Iran's Ghalibaf
stories.description: The president identified Iran's parliament speaker as his counterpart in talks, while Ghalibaf denied any negotiations and threatened U.S. forces.

stories.title: NATO intercepts fourth Iranian missile over Turkey
stories.description: Turkey confirmed a fourth interception Monday as Ankara balances its NATO defense role with efforts to mediate between Washington and Tehran.

stories.title: Nasdaq adopts fast-entry rule to add large IPOs to index in 15 days
stories.description: The new rules, effective May 1, cut the Nasdaq-100 waiting period from up to a year to just 15 trading days for mega-cap listings like SpaceX.

stories.title: Blumenthal demands SEC records after enforcement chief's exit over Trump-tied cases
stories.description: The senator is seeking communications on crypto enforcement actions involving backers of the Trump family's World Liberty Financial venture.

stories.title: TotalEnergies reaps over $1B from historic Middle East oil bet
stories.description: The French energy giant bought nearly all available Dubai crude cargoes in March, exploiting war-driven market turmoil for record trading profits.

stories.title: Senate panel schedules Warsh Fed chair hearing for April 13
stories.description: The long-delayed confirmation process moves forward despite Sen. Tillis's ongoing blockade over the DOJ investigation into current Chair Powell.

stories.title: Russian oil tanker arrives in Cuba after Trump allows through blockade
stories.description: The sanctioned vessel carrying 730,000 barrels of crude is Cuba's first oil delivery since January, offering temporary relief from a severe energy crisis.

stories.title: Iran war fuels mass protests and deepens splits inside the GOP
stories.description: Organizers estimated 8 million people joined Saturday demonstrations across all 50 states, calling the military campaign "an illegal war."

stories.title: Politico names Jonathan Greenberger as editor in chief
stories.description: The former ABC News Washington bureau chief, who joined Politico in 2024, will succeed co-founder John Harris effective May 1.

stories.title: Iran calls US 15-point peace proposal 'unrealistic' as Trump threatens Kharg Island seizure
stories.description: Tehran denied direct talks with Washington and rejected the plan's terms, while Trump signaled possible seizure of Iran's main oil export hub.

stories.title: Zelensky signs Gulf defense deals as Ukraine-Russia talks remain frozen
stories.description: Ukraine's president secured 10-year defense pacts with Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Qatar while pushing the U.S. to restart trilateral peace talks.

stories.title: North Korea tests upgraded missile engine with higher thrust
stories.description: Kim Jong Un supervised a solid-fuel engine test reaching 2,500 kilonewtons, up from 1,971 kN in September, according to state media.

stories.title: Trump allies launch $100M AI group for midterms
stories.description: Innovation Council Action, a nonprofit led by former White House aide Taylor Budowich and backed by David Sacks, plans to target AI regulation critics.

stories.title: Satellite images show 88 structures destroyed at key Iran missile site
stories.description: A month into Operation Epic Fury, the U.S. has confirmed destroying only about a third of Iran's missile arsenal despite thousands of strikes.

stories.title: Iran hits Abu Dhabi smelter, Saudi base as war widens across Gulf
stories.description: Iran struck targets across the Persian Gulf on Saturday, damaging a major UAE aluminum plant and wounding 12 U.S. troops at a Saudi air base.

stories.title: Trump weighs sending troops into Iran to seize uranium
stories.description: The Wall Street Journal reported Sunday that the president is open to deploying special operations forces to extract nearly 1,000 pounds of enriched uranium.

stories.title: Vance wins CPAC 2028 straw poll as Rubio surges
stories.description: Secretary of State Rubio jumped from 3% last year to 35%, while Vice President Vance's support fell from 61% to 53%.

stories.title: FBI moves to release Swalwell files from closed spy probe
stories.description: Director Kash Patel ordered agents to prepare decade-old investigative documents on Rep. Eric Swalwell tied to a suspected Chinese operative.

stories.title: GOP Rep. Mace demands Congress approve any ground troop deployment to Iran
stories.description: Top House Intelligence Democrat Himes accused Trump of fabricating Iran negotiation claims to calm financial markets.

stories.title: Houthis launch second attack on Israel within 24 hours, vow more strikes
stories.description: Yemen's Iran-aligned rebels formally entered the month-old Mideast war, firing missiles and drones at southern Israel in two waves on Saturday.

stories.title: Rosatom says Bushehr nuclear plant situation worsening after third strike
stories.description: Russia's state nuclear agency warned Saturday that conditions at Iran's only operational nuclear power plant continue to deteriorate amid repeated attacks.

stories.title: Homan says ICE will stay at airports even after TSA pay resumes
stories.description: Border czar Tom Homan said Sunday that ICE agents will remain at airports until operations fully normalize, even as TSA paychecks are expected Monday.

stories.title: Drone strike hits Kurdistan president's home in Duhok
stories.description: Iraq's PM ordered a joint federal-Kurdish investigation after a drone targeted Nechirvan Barzani's residence Saturday. No casualties were reported.

stories.title: Iran demands UN accountability over strikes on oil infrastructure
stories.description: Tehran's UN ambassador filed formal complaints over March 7 and 18 attacks on energy facilities, calling them ecocide and demanding reparations.

stories.title: DHS watchdog probes FEMA contractor tied to Noem, Lewandowski
stories.description: Kara Voorhies wielded broad influence over FEMA contracts but appeared on no organizational chart, according to The Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: Poland extends border checks with Germany, Lithuania until October
stories.description: The interior ministry said Saturday the controls are needed to combat illegal migration and maintain internal security.

stories.title: USS Gerald R. Ford arrives in Croatia for repairs after Red Sea fire
stories.description: The carrier anchored in Split on Saturday after a laundry room fire injured sailors and displaced hundreds during its nine-month deployment.

stories.title: Trump threatens to 'expose' GOP senators opposing filibuster end amid DHS shutdown
stories.description: As the partial DHS shutdown hit day 43, the president escalated pressure on his own party after Congress left for a two-week recess without a deal.

stories.title: Vance says UFOs are 'demons,' vows to investigate files
stories.description: The vice president told a podcast host he plans to visit Area 51 and use his remaining three years in office to "get to the bottom" of UFO files.

stories.title: Border Patrol agent indicted for strangling handcuffed detainee
stories.description: Eduardo Prat also faces a charge for allegedly concealing body camera footage of the 2023 assault in southern New Mexico.

stories.title: Trump claims Nobel snub, calls himself a peacemaker amid Iran war
stories.description: At a Saudi-backed summit in Miami Beach, the president said he stopped eight wars and criticized Norway's Nobel committee while defending the conflict with Iran.

stories.title: Explosion hits near Erbil airport as Kurdistan attacks persist
stories.description: Saturday's blast came a day after the US and Iraq formed a joint security committee to prevent attacks from Iraqi territory.

stories.title: Judge dismisses DOJ suit over Minnesota tuition for undocumented students
stories.description: The ruling found the federal government misread a 1996 law and failed to prove the programs discriminated against U.S. citizens.

stories.title: 'No Kings' protests sweep Europe as London draws half a million
stories.description: Demonstrators marched in cities across Europe and beyond Saturday, extending the American anti-Trump movement into a global day of action.

stories.title: IRGC claims US Marines killed in strike on Kuwait
stories.description: Iran says its forces hit American positions on Bubiyan Island with drones and missiles, but the Pentagon has not confirmed casualties.

stories.title: Judge drops charges against officers in Breonna Taylor case
stories.description: The dismissal with prejudice means the two former Louisville officers can never be reprosecuted for their role in the fatal 2020 raid.

stories.title: Law firms urge appeals court to block Trump orders
stories.description: Four major firms filed briefs Friday arguing executive orders punishing them for their legal work are unconstitutional retaliation.

stories.title: Iran war wipes $11.5 trillion from global markets in one month
stories.description: The conflict's closure of the Strait of Hormuz has sent oil prices surging roughly 45% and left investors with few safe havens.

stories.title: Japan's Kikai supervolcano is refilling with magma, study finds
stories.description: Researchers mapped a 220-cubic-kilometer magma reservoir beneath the caldera that produced the Holocene's largest eruption 7,300 years ago.

stories.title: Trump weighs stripping NATO voting rights over Iran war dispute
stories.description: The president is considering a "pay-to-play" model that could exclude allies spending less than 5% of GDP on defense from key decisions.

stories.title: Medvedev warns nuclear conflict between Israel and Iran is now 'inevitable'
stories.description: Russia's deputy Security Council chairman blamed U.S. and Israeli military action for accelerating nuclear proliferation risks across the Middle East.

stories.title: DC airports grounded again after odor forces evacuation of air traffic facility
stories.description: The Potomac TRACON in Virginia was evacuated Friday evening for the second time in two weeks, halting flights at five airports.

stories.title: Japan backs US deep sea mining push in new pact
stories.description: The non-binding memorandum, signed after a Trump-Takaichi meeting, was circulated at a UN seabed authority session where 40 nations seek a mining moratorium.

stories.title: Poland's Tusk warns of imminent escalation in Iran war
stories.description: The Polish prime minister cited allied intelligence suggesting the conflict will intensify, not stabilize, in the coming days.

stories.title: Iran fires cluster munitions at Tel Aviv, killing one
stories.description: A day of dramatic escalation saw Israel strike two Iranian nuclear facilities for the first time in the war, prompting retaliatory missile fire that killed a man in Tel Aviv.

stories.title: Trump floats renaming Strait of Hormuz after himself
stories.description: At a Saudi-backed investor summit in Miami Beach, the president jokingly called the contested waterway the "Strait of Trump" — but aides say the idea isn't entirely a joke.

stories.title: Judge threatens VA with contempt over union contract defiance
stories.description: The agency re-terminated a collective bargaining agreement covering 320,000 workers hours before a compliance hearing, the judge said.

stories.title: Rapper Balendra Shah sworn in as Nepal's youngest PM
stories.description: The 35-year-old former mayor of Kathmandu is also the first person from the Madhesi community to lead the Himalayan nation.

stories.title: Trump unveils farm aid at White House amid tariff, war pressures
stories.description: The president announced new SBA loan guarantees and biofuel standards before more than 800 farmers as the sector struggles with rising costs.

stories.title: Iran agrees to let humanitarian aid pass through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The first breakthrough at the contested waterway in a month of war came as the UN launched a task force to protect global fertilizer trade.

stories.title: Hezbollah confirms more than 400 fighters killed as Israel conflict grows
stories.description: The first comprehensive casualty tally emerges as Israel claims an even higher toll of 700 in its expanding air and ground campaign in Lebanon.

stories.title: Trump presses Saudi Arabia on Abraham Accords at Miami summit
stories.description: The president used his closing address at the FII PRIORITY summit to again urge Riyadh to normalize relations with Israel amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

stories.title: China protests after scholar's suicide tied to US interrogation
stories.description: Beijing formally protested to Washington and demanded a full investigation after a Chinese postdoctoral researcher died by suicide following law enforcement questioning.

stories.title: IRGC claims destruction of US refueling fleet at Al-Kharj base
stories.description: Iran says its 84th wave of strikes hit aerial refueling aircraft and logistical assets at the key Saudi facility supporting U.S. operations.

stories.title: Judge presses DOJ over FBI seizure of Fulton County 2020 ballots
stories.description: A Trump-appointed federal judge heard arguments Friday as Fulton County fights to recover more than 630 boxes of election materials seized in January.

stories.title: China and Pakistan agree to jointly push for ceasefire in Iran war
stories.description: Wang Yi and Ishaq Dar aligned on an immediate ceasefire as Pakistan relays indirect talks between Washington and Tehran over a 15-point U.S. proposal.

stories.title: Iran death toll tops 1,900 as IFRC warns of deepening crisis
stories.description: The Red Cross federation said Friday that at least 20,000 have been injured since U.S.-Israeli strikes began on Feb. 28, with relief efforts strained.

stories.title: Trump takes aim at Cuba in closing address at Miami summit
stories.description: Havana has turned to Pope Leo XIV to mediate with Washington as a U.S. oil embargo pushes the island toward economic collapse.

stories.title: Iran strikes Israel, Gulf states as IRGC warns civilians to flee US bases
stories.description: On the 28th day of the war, Iran launched missiles and drones at Israel, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and the UAE as Israel vowed to escalate strikes on Tehran.

stories.title: Gas prices near $4 as states split on tax holidays
stories.description: The national average hit $3.98, up a dollar in one month, spurring federal and state gas tax suspension proposals with mixed results.

stories.title: Hegseth struck Black and female officers from Army promotion list
stories.description: The defense secretary overrode Army leaders' objections, removing two Black and two female officers from a one-star general list, according to the New York Times.

stories.title: Mexico says 40,000 of 130,000 missing may be alive
stories.description: President Sheinbaum presented a long-awaited report on Mexico's disappearance crisis, revealing a national database plagued by gaps, duplicates, and missing data.

stories.title: Iran-linked hackers breach FBI director's personal email
stories.description: The Handala Hack Team posted photos and documents from Kash Patel's Gmail account; the DOJ confirmed the materials appear authentic.

stories.title: G7 agrees to multinational Hormuz escort mission after Iran war ends
stories.description: Foreign ministers meeting in France committed to restoring freedom of navigation in the strait, with over 30 nations signaling readiness to contribute.

stories.title: DOJ issues subpoenas in Paramount-Warner Bros merger probe
stories.description: Federal investigators are examining the $110 billion deal's impact on streaming competition, content rights, and movie theaters.

stories.title: RFK Jr. ally says White House now sees vaccines as 'losing issue'
stories.description: A federal court ruling, a key adviser's resignation, and growing Senate resistance are forcing the administration to retreat from Kennedy's vaccine overhaul.

stories.title: US land mines found near Shiraz kill civilians in first such deployment in decades
stories.description: Weapons experts identified American-made BLU-91/B anti-tank mines scattered near an Iranian missile base, drawing condemnation from human rights groups.

stories.title: Controllers say LaGuardia needed more staff night of deadly crash
stories.description: Six air traffic controllers told Reuters the airport's workload on March 22 warranted additional staffing before the fatal collision.

stories.title: House ethics panel finds Rep. Cherfilus-McCormick guilty of 25 violations
stories.description: A bipartisan committee ruled the Florida Democrat funneled $5 million in COVID-19 relief funds into her 2022 campaign, setting up a possible expulsion vote.

stories.title: Deployed nuclear warheads rose again in 2025, report finds
stories.description: The annual Nuclear Weapons Ban Monitor counted 4,012 warheads on missiles, submarines, and bomber bases, up 108 from 2024.

stories.title: Iran accuses US-Israel of genocide at UN Human Rights Council
stories.description: Foreign Minister Araghchi addressed an emergency session Friday, calling the Minab school airstrike the "tip of a far larger iceberg" of war crimes.

stories.title: US can confirm only a third of Iran's missiles destroyed
stories.description: Intelligence assessments contradict Trump's claim that Iran has "very few rockets left" as the war nears its one-month mark.

stories.title: SpaceX IPO sparks $15M in ticker bets before filing
stories.description: Polymarket wagers on SpaceX's ticker symbol, valuation, and exchange have topped $15.2 million even though the company has yet to file IPO paperwork.

stories.title: Buy America law stalls affordable housing amid HUD waiver backlog
stories.description: Developers say HUD is taking six months or more to process waivers, adding hundreds of thousands of dollars in costs to federally funded projects.

stories.title: IRGC urges civilians to flee areas near US forces as Trump extends Hormuz deadline
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and threatened US military positions as Trump pushed his strike deadline to April 6.

stories.title: Asia-Pacific governments rush to calm markets as Middle East war drives energy crisis
stories.description: From emergency budgets to fuel rationing plans, countries across the region are deploying fiscal packages and liquidity measures to counter the economic fallout.

stories.title: Kremlin denies Putin asked oligarchs to fund Ukraine war costs
stories.description: Spokesman Peskov said a billionaire volunteered a large donation on his own, contradicting reports that Putin directly appealed for budget contributions.

stories.title: US confirms killing of IRGC Navy chief Tangsiri in Bandar Abbas strike
stories.description: CENTCOM verified the death of the architect of Iran's Strait of Hormuz blockade, calling on remaining IRGC Navy members to abandon their posts.

stories.title: Study finds cells, firms and cities all slow in adding new functions as they grow
stories.description: Researchers identified a shared mathematical pattern governing how complex systems diversify, from bacteria to corporations to government agencies.

stories.title: Rubio calls Ukraine 'not America's war' as peace talks stall
stories.description: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio's pointed remark at the G7 came as Russia launched a record 948-drone barrage and no new peace talks are scheduled.

stories.title: US burns through 850-plus Tomahawks in Iran war, alarming Pentagon
stories.description: More than 20% of the estimated U.S. Tomahawk stockpile has been expended in four weeks of strikes, the Washington Post reported.

stories.title: ECB survey shows Iran war upended eurozone inflation outlook
stories.description: Consumer confidence across Europe hit its lowest since October 2023 as the Middle East conflict drives energy prices higher and revives inflation fears.

stories.title: Epstein survivors sue Trump admin and Google over exposed identities
stories.description: About 100 victims filed a class action alleging the DOJ's mass document release exposed their identities and Google refused to remove the information.

stories.title: China launches two trade barrier probes against U.S.
stories.description: Beijing's Commerce Ministry opened investigations into U.S. supply chain disruptions and green trade restrictions, retaliating against Washington's Section 301 probes.

stories.title: US has lost at least 16 aircraft in Iran war as losses mount
stories.description: The toll includes an F-35A damaged by Iranian ground fire on March 19 — the first time the stealth jet has been struck by enemy fire.

stories.title: Mediators say Iran never requested strike pause Trump cited
stories.description: President Trump extended the deadline for energy strikes to April 6, but mediators told the WSJ that Tehran made no such request.

stories.title: Anthropic discusses Q4 2026 IPO that could raise over $60B
stories.description: Bankers competing for the mandate expect the AI startup's listing to rank among the largest IPOs in history, according to The Information.

stories.title: Iran claims 1 million fighters ready for US ground invasion
stories.description: Tehran says volunteers are flooding Basij and IRGC centers as the U.S. sends 82nd Airborne paratroopers and Marines to the region.

stories.title: Experts warn Iran war's environmental toll could persist for decades
stories.description: Over 400 environmentally harmful incidents have been logged since the conflict began, with toxic pollution threatening air, water, and soil across the Gulf.

stories.title: UK pledges £100M more for Ukraine's air defenses
stories.description: The additional funding brings Britain's air defense contributions to roughly £600 million over two months as Russia's aerial attacks persist.

stories.title: Pentagon confirms first use of drone boats in Iran conflict
stories.description: Uncrewed speedboats built by BlackSea Technologies have been patrolling in support of Operation Epic Fury, the Pentagon said Thursday.

stories.title: Houthi leader signals readiness to join Iran war against US and Israel
stories.description: Ansarallah has refrained from entering the conflict since it began Feb. 28 but controls the strategic Bab al-Mandeb Strait.

stories.title: Education Dept. to vacate headquarters for smaller office amid dismantling push
stories.description: The move, planned for August, comes as the agency's building sits 70% vacant after more than a year of mass layoffs under the Trump administration.

stories.title: Iran offers Spain Hormuz access in first such EU overture
stories.description: Tehran cited Madrid's "commitment to international law" after Spain barred US forces from using its bases for strikes on Iran.

stories.title: Bipartisan Senate bill targets insider trading on prediction markets
stories.description: Four senators introduced legislation Thursday to ban government officials from using nonpublic information to trade on prediction market platforms.

stories.title: US senators introduce bill to sanction Hungary over Ukraine aid blockade
stories.description: Sens. Shaheen and Tillis unveiled the bipartisan Block Putin Act targeting Hungarian officials as Orban continues to hold up a €90 billion EU loan to Kyiv.

top.title: NASA chief says 90% chance of proving ancient life on Mars
top.description: Jared Isaacman pointed to the universe's scale and recent Perseverance rover findings as reasons for optimism about microbial evidence on Mars.

top.title: Trump claims Iran requested ceasefire; Tehran denies it
top.description: Hours before a planned primetime address on the war, Trump and Iran offered starkly conflicting accounts of ceasefire talks.

top.title: Iran hits tanker off Qatar, strikes Kuwait airport as Gulf war widens
top.description: Qatar said it intercepted two of three cruise missiles from Iran, but a third struck a QatarEnergy-chartered tanker. No casualties were reported.

top.title: NASA set to launch Artemis II moon mission today
top.description: The first crewed lunar voyage since 1972 is targeting liftoff at 6:24 p.m. EDT from Kennedy Space Center with four astronauts aboard.

top.title: South Korea exports hit record $86B as global factories stockpile amid Iran war
top.description: Semiconductor shipments surged over 150%, while euro zone and U.S. factory activity beat forecasts as firms panic-bought materials ahead of further disruptions.

top.title: Qubits needed to crack encryption fall 200-fold in under a year
top.description: Three papers have slashed the estimated quantum computing power needed to break RSA-2048 from 20 million qubits to under 100,000, accelerating the threat to modern encryption.

top.title: Global stocks surge as Trump signals end to Iran war
top.description: European and Indian markets rallied sharply on Wednesday after Trump said U.S. forces could withdraw from Iran within weeks.

top.title: Parker Solar Probe data challenges models of Sun's magnetic engine
top.description: Protons and heavy ions behave differently during solar magnetic reconnection, contradicting models that assumed uniform particle acceleration.

top.title: USTR flags Canadian alcohol bans, 'Buy Canadian' as trade barriers
top.description: The annual report demands American liquor "immediately and permanently" return to provincial shelves ahead of the USMCA's first formal review in July.

top.title: New papers slash qubit estimates for codebreaking
top.description: The March 31 papers sharply cut projected quantum resources needed to crack elliptic curve encryption, intensifying pressure to adopt post-quantum safeguards.

top.title: DHS pauses warehouse purchases for immigrant detention
top.description: New Secretary Mullin is reviewing the $38.3 billion detention expansion program launched by his predecessor, Kristi Noem.

top.title: Forest Service to move headquarters to Salt Lake City in sweeping overhaul
top.description: The USDA announced the agency's nine regional offices will close and be replaced by 15 state directors under a new organizational model.

top.title: China and Pakistan propose ceasefire as Chinese ships transit Hormuz
top.description: Beijing confirmed three Chinese vessels crossed the strait after coordinating with Tehran, as China and Pakistan unveiled a five-point peace plan.

top.title: Netanyahu says Israel destroying Iran's weapons capability
top.description: In a Passover eve address, the prime minister said Israel had inflicted "10 plagues" on Iran and its regional proxy networks.

top.title: USS Bush strike group deploys as Hegseth calls Iran war days 'decisive'
top.description: The carrier left Norfolk on Tuesday as the Pentagon said U.S. forces have struck over 11,000 targets in the one-month-old campaign against Iran.

top.title: Syria vows to stay out of US-Israeli war on Iran unless attacked
top.description: President Ahmed al-Sharaa declared neutrality in a London speech, saying Syria has "had enough war" as the conflict enters its second month.

top.title: UN warns Lebanon at 'breaking point' as 1.1M displaced
top.description: Emergency Relief Coordinator Tom Fletcher told the Security Council that over 1,240 people have been killed in four weeks as Israeli operations intensify.

top.title: Trump says Strait of Hormuz will 'automatically' reopen when US exits Iran war
top.description: The president told the New York Post the war "won't last much longer," but Iran denied any negotiations are underway to end the conflict.

top.title: Judge blocks deal that would let churches endorse candidates
top.description: A federal judge in Texas rejected a proposed IRS settlement that would have exempted houses of worship from the Johnson Amendment's ban on political endorsements.

top.title: FDA moves to allow banned peptides backed by RFK Jr.
top.description: The agency is expected to reclassify more than a dozen injectable peptides restricted in 2023, restoring access through compounding pharmacies.

top.title: Explosions rock Damascus as Israel intercepts Iranian missiles over Syria
top.description: Syrian state TV reported blasts across the capital on Tuesday as Israeli air defenses shot down Iranian projectiles crossing Syrian airspace.

top.title: Rubio directs US embassies to use X and military psyops against propaganda
top.description: The diplomatic cable instructs staff worldwide to coordinate with Pentagon psychological operations and leverage Elon Musk's platform.

top.title: Judge orders UPenn to hand over Jewish employee records
top.description: The ruling gives Penn until May 1 to comply with the subpoena but bars disclosure of individuals' ties to specific Jewish organizations.

top.title: Bipartisan senators introduce bill to boost Taiwan drone production
top.description: The legislation would create a U.S.-Taiwan working group to co-produce drones and build supply chains independent from China.

top.title: Trump signs order restricting mail-in voting ahead of midterms
top.description: The executive order directs federal agencies to limit absentee ballot access, building on prior election actions largely blocked by courts.

top.title: Fourth Greek tanker crosses Hormuz despite Iran blockade
top.description: Dynacom Tankers sent the Suezmax Pola through the strait with its transponder off, carrying 1 million barrels of crude to Thailand.

top.title: Pentagon says B-52s now flying over Iran after 11,000 strikes in 30 days
top.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth and Gen. Caine said near-total destruction of Iran's air defenses enabled the first overland bomber missions.

top.title: Majority disapprove of Trump's signature on dollar bills, YouGov poll finds
top.description: The survey found 48% strongly oppose replacing the U.S. Treasurer's signature with the president's on new paper currency.

top.title: Supreme Court strikes down Colorado conversion therapy ban in 8-1 ruling
top.description: The decision in Chiles v. Salazar requires courts to apply heightened First Amendment scrutiny to laws restricting talk therapy for minors.

top.title: Hegseth calls upcoming days 'decisive' in Iran war
top.description: The defense secretary pointed to mass desertions and degraded Iranian military capabilities as signs the conflict is nearing a turning point.

top.title: Judge permanently blocks Trump order defunding NPR, PBS
top.description: U.S. District Judge Randolph Moss ruled the executive order violated the First Amendment by punishing the outlets over speech the president dislikes.

top.title: Vance to publish book on Catholic faith while in office
top.description: "Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith," due June 16, traces the vice president's journey from atheism to Catholicism.

top.title: Global banks turn to Chinese stocks as Iran war roils markets
top.description: Reuters reports major banks are treating Chinese equities as a safe haven, with renewable energy stocks surging even as global indexes tumble.

top.title: Judge halts Trump's White House ballroom construction
top.description: U.S. District Judge Richard Leon ruled that "no statute" authorizes the $400 million project, ordering work to stop unless Congress approves it.

top.title: Australia, Indonesia probe tech giants over under-16 bans
top.description: Both countries are investigating major platforms for failing to enforce social media age restrictions, with fines and sanctions on the table.

top.title: Data centers warm nearby land by up to 16°F, Cambridge study finds
top.description: Satellite data from roughly 6,000 facilities worldwide shows AI data centers create "heat islands" affecting an estimated 340 million people.

top.title: Trump warns European allies after Italy, France block US military access
top.description: Italy denied US bombers use of a Sicilian base, and France blocked Israeli overflights carrying American weapons, deepening a NATO rift over the Iran war.

top.title: Pentagon denies Hegseth sought defense investments before Iran war
top.description: The Financial Times reported that Hegseth's broker sought to invest millions in a defense ETF weeks before the Feb. 28 strike on Iran.

top.title: NYU closes Abu Dhabi campus after Iran threatens US universities
top.description: The IRGC declared American university campuses across the Gulf "legitimate targets," setting a Monday morning deadline for Washington to condemn strikes on Iranian schools.

top.title: Noem's final day at DHS arrives amid IG probe, spending scandal
top.description: Markwayne Mullin takes over as secretary while investigators examine contracts and lavish ad spending tied to Noem and adviser Corey Lewandowski.

top.title: Health groups sue EPA over mercury rule rollback for coal plants
top.description: A coalition of two dozen organizations filed suit in D.C. Circuit court, arguing the repeal of tighter emissions limits endangers children and communities.

top.title: Zelensky says Russia exploiting Iran war to fund its fight against Ukraine
top.description: In an exclusive interview, the Ukrainian president accused Moscow of sharing satellite imagery with Iran and profiting from loosened oil sanctions.

top.title: Trump unveils 47-story skyscraper design for Miami presidential library
top.description: The towering glass tower would break sharply from the low-slung, campus-style buildings that have defined presidential libraries for decades.

top.title: Iran says ambassador will stay in Beirut despite Lebanon's expulsion order
top.description: Tehran confirmed Monday its envoy remains in Lebanon past the March 29 departure deadline, deepening a diplomatic standoff backed by Hezbollah.

top.title: Ukraine says Russia recruited 27,000 from 135 countries
top.description: Ukrainian intelligence released data identifying 27,407 foreign nationals recruited into Russia's armed forces from 135 countries.

top.title: US struck Isfahan ammo depot with bunker busters
top.description: Trump shared footage of the massive secondary explosions on Truth Social as the month-old U.S.-Israeli campaign surpasses 15,000 targets struck.

top.title: FBI labels Temple Isreal attack Hezbollah-inspired terrorism
top.description: The bureau said Ayman Ghazali planned the March 12 ramming and shooting at Temple Israel to "kill as many" people as possible.

top.title: Manhattan prosecutors probe insider trading on prediction markets
top.description: The SDNY recently met with Polymarket to discuss how fraud and insider trading laws apply to suspicious bets on the fast-growing platforms.

top.title: White House urges Congress back from recess amid DHS shutdown
top.description: Trump urged Congress to cancel Easter recess and return to Washington, offering to host lawmakers for dinner at the White House.

top.title: Israel Knesset passes death penalty law for Palestinians
top.description: Military courts must now impose a mandatory death sentence for terrorism-related killings, with executions by hanging within 90 days of sentencing.

top.title: NYT identifies untested U.S. missile in deadly Iran civilian strike
top.description: A New York Times investigation found the Pentagon used a never-before-deployed Precision Strike Missile in a Feb. 28 strike that killed at least 21 people.

top.title: Trump floats asking Arab states to help pay for Iran war
top.description: The White House said Monday the president is interested in having Gulf allies share the financial burden of the conflict, now in its fifth week.

top.title: Washington governor signs millionaires tax into law
top.description: The 9.9% levy on household income above $1 million is the state's first personal income tax in nearly a century.

top.title: Renoir, Cezanne, Matisse paintings stolen in 3-minute Italian museum heist
top.description: Four masked thieves took three masterpieces worth an estimated €9 million from a private museum near Parma; investigators are reviewing surveillance footage.

top.title: Trump confirms US is negotiating with Iran's Ghalibaf
top.description: The president identified Iran's parliament speaker as his counterpart in talks, while Ghalibaf denied any negotiations and threatened U.S. forces.

top.title: NATO intercepts fourth Iranian missile over Turkey
top.description: Turkey confirmed a fourth interception Monday as Ankara balances its NATO defense role with efforts to mediate between Washington and Tehran.

top.title: Nasdaq adopts fast-entry rule to add large IPOs to index in 15 days
top.description: The new rules, effective May 1, cut the Nasdaq-100 waiting period from up to a year to just 15 trading days for mega-cap listings like SpaceX.

top.title: Blumenthal demands SEC records after enforcement chief's exit over Trump-tied cases
top.description: The senator is seeking communications on crypto enforcement actions involving backers of the Trump family's World Liberty Financial venture.

top.title: TotalEnergies reaps over $1B from historic Middle East oil bet
top.description: The French energy giant bought nearly all available Dubai crude cargoes in March, exploiting war-driven market turmoil for record trading profits.

top.title: Senate panel schedules Warsh Fed chair hearing for April 13
top.description: The long-delayed confirmation process moves forward despite Sen. Tillis's ongoing blockade over the DOJ investigation into current Chair Powell.

top.title: Russian oil tanker arrives in Cuba after Trump allows through blockade
top.description: The sanctioned vessel carrying 730,000 barrels of crude is Cuba's first oil delivery since January, offering temporary relief from a severe energy crisis.

top.title: Iran war fuels mass protests and deepens splits inside the GOP
top.description: Organizers estimated 8 million people joined Saturday demonstrations across all 50 states, calling the military campaign "an illegal war."

top.title: Politico names Jonathan Greenberger as editor in chief
top.description: The former ABC News Washington bureau chief, who joined Politico in 2024, will succeed co-founder John Harris effective May 1.

top.title: Iran calls US 15-point peace proposal 'unrealistic' as Trump threatens Kharg Island seizure
top.description: Tehran denied direct talks with Washington and rejected the plan's terms, while Trump signaled possible seizure of Iran's main oil export hub.

top.title: Zelensky signs Gulf defense deals as Ukraine-Russia talks remain frozen
top.description: Ukraine's president secured 10-year defense pacts with Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Qatar while pushing the U.S. to restart trilateral peace talks.

top.title: North Korea tests upgraded missile engine with higher thrust
top.description: Kim Jong Un supervised a solid-fuel engine test reaching 2,500 kilonewtons, up from 1,971 kN in September, according to state media.

top.title: Trump allies launch $100M AI group for midterms
top.description: Innovation Council Action, a nonprofit led by former White House aide Taylor Budowich and backed by David Sacks, plans to target AI regulation critics.

top.title: Satellite images show 88 structures destroyed at key Iran missile site
top.description: A month into Operation Epic Fury, the U.S. has confirmed destroying only about a third of Iran's missile arsenal despite thousands of strikes.

top.title: Iran hits Abu Dhabi smelter, Saudi base as war widens across Gulf
top.description: Iran struck targets across the Persian Gulf on Saturday, damaging a major UAE aluminum plant and wounding 12 U.S. troops at a Saudi air base.

top.title: Trump weighs sending troops into Iran to seize uranium
top.description: The Wall Street Journal reported Sunday that the president is open to deploying special operations forces to extract nearly 1,000 pounds of enriched uranium.

top.title: Vance wins CPAC 2028 straw poll as Rubio surges
top.description: Secretary of State Rubio jumped from 3% last year to 35%, while Vice President Vance's support fell from 61% to 53%.

top.title: FBI moves to release Swalwell files from closed spy probe
top.description: Director Kash Patel ordered agents to prepare decade-old investigative documents on Rep. Eric Swalwell tied to a suspected Chinese operative.

top.title: GOP Rep. Mace demands Congress approve any ground troop deployment to Iran
top.description: Top House Intelligence Democrat Himes accused Trump of fabricating Iran negotiation claims to calm financial markets.

top.title: Houthis launch second attack on Israel within 24 hours, vow more strikes
top.description: Yemen's Iran-aligned rebels formally entered the month-old Mideast war, firing missiles and drones at southern Israel in two waves on Saturday.

top.title: Rosatom says Bushehr nuclear plant situation worsening after third strike
top.description: Russia's state nuclear agency warned Saturday that conditions at Iran's only operational nuclear power plant continue to deteriorate amid repeated attacks.

top.title: Homan says ICE will stay at airports even after TSA pay resumes
top.description: Border czar Tom Homan said Sunday that ICE agents will remain at airports until operations fully normalize, even as TSA paychecks are expected Monday.

top.title: Drone strike hits Kurdistan president's home in Duhok
top.description: Iraq's PM ordered a joint federal-Kurdish investigation after a drone targeted Nechirvan Barzani's residence Saturday. No casualties were reported.

top.title: Iran demands UN accountability over strikes on oil infrastructure
top.description: Tehran's UN ambassador filed formal complaints over March 7 and 18 attacks on energy facilities, calling them ecocide and demanding reparations.

top.title: DHS watchdog probes FEMA contractor tied to Noem, Lewandowski
top.description: Kara Voorhies wielded broad influence over FEMA contracts but appeared on no organizational chart, according to The Wall Street Journal.

top.title: Poland extends border checks with Germany, Lithuania until October
top.description: The interior ministry said Saturday the controls are needed to combat illegal migration and maintain internal security.

top.title: USS Gerald R. Ford arrives in Croatia for repairs after Red Sea fire
top.description: The carrier anchored in Split on Saturday after a laundry room fire injured sailors and displaced hundreds during its nine-month deployment.

top.title: Trump threatens to 'expose' GOP senators opposing filibuster end amid DHS shutdown
top.description: As the partial DHS shutdown hit day 43, the president escalated pressure on his own party after Congress left for a two-week recess without a deal.

top.title: Vance says UFOs are 'demons,' vows to investigate files
top.description: The vice president told a podcast host he plans to visit Area 51 and use his remaining three years in office to "get to the bottom" of UFO files.

top.title: Border Patrol agent indicted for strangling handcuffed detainee
top.description: Eduardo Prat also faces a charge for allegedly concealing body camera footage of the 2023 assault in southern New Mexico.

top.title: Trump claims Nobel snub, calls himself a peacemaker amid Iran war
top.description: At a Saudi-backed summit in Miami Beach, the president said he stopped eight wars and criticized Norway's Nobel committee while defending the conflict with Iran.

top.title: Explosion hits near Erbil airport as Kurdistan attacks persist
top.description: Saturday's blast came a day after the US and Iraq formed a joint security committee to prevent attacks from Iraqi territory.

top.title: Judge dismisses DOJ suit over Minnesota tuition for undocumented students
top.description: The ruling found the federal government misread a 1996 law and failed to prove the programs discriminated against U.S. citizens.

top.title: 'No Kings' protests sweep Europe as London draws half a million
top.description: Demonstrators marched in cities across Europe and beyond Saturday, extending the American anti-Trump movement into a global day of action.

top.title: IRGC claims US Marines killed in strike on Kuwait
top.description: Iran says its forces hit American positions on Bubiyan Island with drones and missiles, but the Pentagon has not confirmed casualties.

top.title: Judge drops charges against officers in Breonna Taylor case
top.description: The dismissal with prejudice means the two former Louisville officers can never be reprosecuted for their role in the fatal 2020 raid.

top.title: Law firms urge appeals court to block Trump orders
top.description: Four major firms filed briefs Friday arguing executive orders punishing them for their legal work are unconstitutional retaliation.

top.title: Iran war wipes $11.5 trillion from global markets in one month
top.description: The conflict's closure of the Strait of Hormuz has sent oil prices surging roughly 45% and left investors with few safe havens.

top.title: Japan's Kikai supervolcano is refilling with magma, study finds
top.description: Researchers mapped a 220-cubic-kilometer magma reservoir beneath the caldera that produced the Holocene's largest eruption 7,300 years ago.

top.title: Trump weighs stripping NATO voting rights over Iran war dispute
top.description: The president is considering a "pay-to-play" model that could exclude allies spending less than 5% of GDP on defense from key decisions.

top.title: Medvedev warns nuclear conflict between Israel and Iran is now 'inevitable'
top.description: Russia's deputy Security Council chairman blamed U.S. and Israeli military action for accelerating nuclear proliferation risks across the Middle East.

top.title: DC airports grounded again after odor forces evacuation of air traffic facility
top.description: The Potomac TRACON in Virginia was evacuated Friday evening for the second time in two weeks, halting flights at five airports.

top.title: Japan backs US deep sea mining push in new pact
top.description: The non-binding memorandum, signed after a Trump-Takaichi meeting, was circulated at a UN seabed authority session where 40 nations seek a mining moratorium.

top.title: Poland's Tusk warns of imminent escalation in Iran war
top.description: The Polish prime minister cited allied intelligence suggesting the conflict will intensify, not stabilize, in the coming days.

top.title: Iran fires cluster munitions at Tel Aviv, killing one
top.description: A day of dramatic escalation saw Israel strike two Iranian nuclear facilities for the first time in the war, prompting retaliatory missile fire that killed a man in Tel Aviv.

top.title: Trump floats renaming Strait of Hormuz after himself
top.description: At a Saudi-backed investor summit in Miami Beach, the president jokingly called the contested waterway the "Strait of Trump" — but aides say the idea isn't entirely a joke.

top.title: Judge threatens VA with contempt over union contract defiance
top.description: The agency re-terminated a collective bargaining agreement covering 320,000 workers hours before a compliance hearing, the judge said.

top.title: Rapper Balendra Shah sworn in as Nepal's youngest PM
top.description: The 35-year-old former mayor of Kathmandu is also the first person from the Madhesi community to lead the Himalayan nation.

top.title: Trump unveils farm aid at White House amid tariff, war pressures
top.description: The president announced new SBA loan guarantees and biofuel standards before more than 800 farmers as the sector struggles with rising costs.

top.title: Iran agrees to let humanitarian aid pass through Strait of Hormuz
top.description: The first breakthrough at the contested waterway in a month of war came as the UN launched a task force to protect global fertilizer trade.

top.title: Hezbollah confirms more than 400 fighters killed as Israel conflict grows
top.description: The first comprehensive casualty tally emerges as Israel claims an even higher toll of 700 in its expanding air and ground campaign in Lebanon.

top.title: Trump presses Saudi Arabia on Abraham Accords at Miami summit
top.description: The president used his closing address at the FII PRIORITY summit to again urge Riyadh to normalize relations with Israel amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

top.title: China protests after scholar's suicide tied to US interrogation
top.description: Beijing formally protested to Washington and demanded a full investigation after a Chinese postdoctoral researcher died by suicide following law enforcement questioning.

top.title: IRGC claims destruction of US refueling fleet at Al-Kharj base
top.description: Iran says its 84th wave of strikes hit aerial refueling aircraft and logistical assets at the key Saudi facility supporting U.S. operations.

top.title: Judge presses DOJ over FBI seizure of Fulton County 2020 ballots
top.description: A Trump-appointed federal judge heard arguments Friday as Fulton County fights to recover more than 630 boxes of election materials seized in January.

top.title: China and Pakistan agree to jointly push for ceasefire in Iran war
top.description: Wang Yi and Ishaq Dar aligned on an immediate ceasefire as Pakistan relays indirect talks between Washington and Tehran over a 15-point U.S. proposal.

top.title: Iran death toll tops 1,900 as IFRC warns of deepening crisis
top.description: The Red Cross federation said Friday that at least 20,000 have been injured since U.S.-Israeli strikes began on Feb. 28, with relief efforts strained.

top.title: Trump takes aim at Cuba in closing address at Miami summit
top.description: Havana has turned to Pope Leo XIV to mediate with Washington as a U.S. oil embargo pushes the island toward economic collapse.

top.title: Iran strikes Israel, Gulf states as IRGC warns civilians to flee US bases
top.description: On the 28th day of the war, Iran launched missiles and drones at Israel, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and the UAE as Israel vowed to escalate strikes on Tehran.

top.title: Gas prices near $4 as states split on tax holidays
top.description: The national average hit $3.98, up a dollar in one month, spurring federal and state gas tax suspension proposals with mixed results.

top.title: Hegseth struck Black and female officers from Army promotion list
top.description: The defense secretary overrode Army leaders' objections, removing two Black and two female officers from a one-star general list, according to the New York Times.

top.title: Mexico says 40,000 of 130,000 missing may be alive
top.description: President Sheinbaum presented a long-awaited report on Mexico's disappearance crisis, revealing a national database plagued by gaps, duplicates, and missing data.

top.title: Iran-linked hackers breach FBI director's personal email
top.description: The Handala Hack Team posted photos and documents from Kash Patel's Gmail account; the DOJ confirmed the materials appear authentic.

top.title: G7 agrees to multinational Hormuz escort mission after Iran war ends
top.description: Foreign ministers meeting in France committed to restoring freedom of navigation in the strait, with over 30 nations signaling readiness to contribute.

top.title: DOJ issues subpoenas in Paramount-Warner Bros merger probe
top.description: Federal investigators are examining the $110 billion deal's impact on streaming competition, content rights, and movie theaters.

top.title: RFK Jr. ally says White House now sees vaccines as 'losing issue'
top.description: A federal court ruling, a key adviser's resignation, and growing Senate resistance are forcing the administration to retreat from Kennedy's vaccine overhaul.

top.title: US land mines found near Shiraz kill civilians in first such deployment in decades
top.description: Weapons experts identified American-made BLU-91/B anti-tank mines scattered near an Iranian missile base, drawing condemnation from human rights groups.

top.title: Controllers say LaGuardia needed more staff night of deadly crash
top.description: Six air traffic controllers told Reuters the airport's workload on March 22 warranted additional staffing before the fatal collision.

top.title: House ethics panel finds Rep. Cherfilus-McCormick guilty of 25 violations
top.description: A bipartisan committee ruled the Florida Democrat funneled $5 million in COVID-19 relief funds into her 2022 campaign, setting up a possible expulsion vote.

top.title: Deployed nuclear warheads rose again in 2025, report finds
top.description: The annual Nuclear Weapons Ban Monitor counted 4,012 warheads on missiles, submarines, and bomber bases, up 108 from 2024.

top.title: Iran accuses US-Israel of genocide at UN Human Rights Council
top.description: Foreign Minister Araghchi addressed an emergency session Friday, calling the Minab school airstrike the "tip of a far larger iceberg" of war crimes.

top.title: US can confirm only a third of Iran's missiles destroyed
top.description: Intelligence assessments contradict Trump's claim that Iran has "very few rockets left" as the war nears its one-month mark.

top.title: SpaceX IPO sparks $15M in ticker bets before filing
top.description: Polymarket wagers on SpaceX's ticker symbol, valuation, and exchange have topped $15.2 million even though the company has yet to file IPO paperwork.

top.title: Buy America law stalls affordable housing amid HUD waiver backlog
top.description: Developers say HUD is taking six months or more to process waivers, adding hundreds of thousands of dollars in costs to federally funded projects.

top.title: IRGC urges civilians to flee areas near US forces as Trump extends Hormuz deadline
top.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and threatened US military positions as Trump pushed his strike deadline to April 6.

top.title: Asia-Pacific governments rush to calm markets as Middle East war drives energy crisis
top.description: From emergency budgets to fuel rationing plans, countries across the region are deploying fiscal packages and liquidity measures to counter the economic fallout.

top.title: Kremlin denies Putin asked oligarchs to fund Ukraine war costs
top.description: Spokesman Peskov said a billionaire volunteered a large donation on his own, contradicting reports that Putin directly appealed for budget contributions.

top.title: US confirms killing of IRGC Navy chief Tangsiri in Bandar Abbas strike
top.description: CENTCOM verified the death of the architect of Iran's Strait of Hormuz blockade, calling on remaining IRGC Navy members to abandon their posts.

top.title: Study finds cells, firms and cities all slow in adding new functions as they grow
top.description: Researchers identified a shared mathematical pattern governing how complex systems diversify, from bacteria to corporations to government agencies.

top.title: Rubio calls Ukraine 'not America's war' as peace talks stall
top.description: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio's pointed remark at the G7 came as Russia launched a record 948-drone barrage and no new peace talks are scheduled.

top.title: US burns through 850-plus Tomahawks in Iran war, alarming Pentagon
top.description: More than 20% of the estimated U.S. Tomahawk stockpile has been expended in four weeks of strikes, the Washington Post reported.

top.title: ECB survey shows Iran war upended eurozone inflation outlook
top.description: Consumer confidence across Europe hit its lowest since October 2023 as the Middle East conflict drives energy prices higher and revives inflation fears.

top.title: Epstein survivors sue Trump admin and Google over exposed identities
top.description: About 100 victims filed a class action alleging the DOJ's mass document release exposed their identities and Google refused to remove the information.

top.title: China launches two trade barrier probes against U.S.
top.description: Beijing's Commerce Ministry opened investigations into U.S. supply chain disruptions and green trade restrictions, retaliating against Washington's Section 301 probes.

top.title: US has lost at least 16 aircraft in Iran war as losses mount
top.description: The toll includes an F-35A damaged by Iranian ground fire on March 19 — the first time the stealth jet has been struck by enemy fire.

top.title: Mediators say Iran never requested strike pause Trump cited
top.description: President Trump extended the deadline for energy strikes to April 6, but mediators told the WSJ that Tehran made no such request.

top.title: Anthropic discusses Q4 2026 IPO that could raise over $60B
top.description: Bankers competing for the mandate expect the AI startup's listing to rank among the largest IPOs in history, according to The Information.

top.title: Iran claims 1 million fighters ready for US ground invasion
top.description: Tehran says volunteers are flooding Basij and IRGC centers as the U.S. sends 82nd Airborne paratroopers and Marines to the region.

top.title: Experts warn Iran war's environmental toll could persist for decades
top.description: Over 400 environmentally harmful incidents have been logged since the conflict began, with toxic pollution threatening air, water, and soil across the Gulf.

top.title: UK pledges £100M more for Ukraine's air defenses
top.description: The additional funding brings Britain's air defense contributions to roughly £600 million over two months as Russia's aerial attacks persist.

top.title: Pentagon confirms first use of drone boats in Iran conflict
top.description: Uncrewed speedboats built by BlackSea Technologies have been patrolling in support of Operation Epic Fury, the Pentagon said Thursday.

top.title: Houthi leader signals readiness to join Iran war against US and Israel
top.description: Ansarallah has refrained from entering the conflict since it began Feb. 28 but controls the strategic Bab al-Mandeb Strait.

top.title: Education Dept. to vacate headquarters for smaller office amid dismantling push
top.description: The move, planned for August, comes as the agency's building sits 70% vacant after more than a year of mass layoffs under the Trump administration.

top.title: Iran offers Spain Hormuz access in first such EU overture
top.description: Tehran cited Madrid's "commitment to international law" after Spain barred US forces from using its bases for strikes on Iran.

top.title: Bipartisan Senate bill targets insider trading on prediction markets
top.description: Four senators introduced legislation Thursday to ban government officials from using nonpublic information to trade on prediction market platforms.

top.title: US senators introduce bill to sanction Hungary over Ukraine aid blockade
top.description: Sens. Shaheen and Tillis unveiled the bipartisan Block Putin Act targeting Hungarian officials as Orban continues to hold up a €90 billion EU loan to Kyiv.
